page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block read speeds reaching 435.08 GB/sec and write speeds at 216.67 GB/sec. Multi-core capabilities are strong, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 24039.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 521.32 GB/sec. The instance also excels in database operations and static web serving, achieving 98.12 GB/sec in static web serving throughput and 47761.21 in PassMark database operations. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall into the average tier, and small-model LLM text generation is relatively weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gn.12xlarge offers high resource density and exceptional memory throughput relative to its ARM-based hardware class, making it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. This server is optimized for high-throughput network applications, database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton processor based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz with dedicated physical cores, it features 192.0 GB of RAM, 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 435.08 GB/sec, and excellent multi-core performance, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 24039.0. It delivers strong results in database operations and static web serving, though single-core performance is average. The instance represents a highly resource-dense option for multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for network-intensive applications, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a 48-core ARM64 server with 192 GB RAM and 150 Gbps bandwidth, built for database and network-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering high-bandwidth memory, 150 Gbps networking, and dedicated physical cores for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 Graviton physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier uncached read and write bandwidth
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Excellent performance for database operations and static web serving
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads and software compilation
